title: senna silvestris var. guaranitica
intro: Senna silvestris var. guaranitica is a variety of the Senna silvestris plant, a flowering plant in the legume family native to South America. It is traditionally used for its laxative properties, derived from its anthraquinone glycosides, which help to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. The medicinal properties of Senna silvestris var. guaranitica have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, and it is also valued for its ability to help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. Herbal preparations of Senna silvestris var. guaranitica can be found in teas, capsules, and infusions, which are often used as a natural remedy for constipation and other gastrointestinal issues.
